UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile within the w3Develops application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

socialLinks.description: An array of social media links associated with the user.

primarySkill.description: User's primary skill for group sorting.

learningPace.description: User's preferred learning pace for group sorting (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

StudyGroup.title: StudyGroup
StudyGroup.description: Represents a study group within the w3Develops application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudyGroup entity.

description.description: Description of the study group's focus or purpose.

memberIds.description: References to UserProfiles. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N StudyGroup)

skill.description: The primary skill associated with this study group.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Path-based ownership ensures only the authenticated user can access their own profile. Includes the user's email, display name, profile picture URL, social links, primary skill, and learning pace.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores study group data, including name, description, and an array of memberIds. The 'memberIds' array facilitates managing group memberships and authorizations. This structure supports listing all study groups.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The unique identifier of the study group.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed for the w3Develops application, prioritizing security, scalability, and ease of debugging. It adheres to the principle of Authorization Independence by denormalizing authorization-related data, avoiding the need for `get()` calls in security rules. It also ensures data clarity with explicit naming and modeling.

**User Profiles:** User profiles are stored under `/users/{userId}/profile`. This path-based ownership is secure, efficient, and simplifies access control. The `userId` parameter directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID.

**Study Groups:** Study groups are stored under `/studyGroups/{groupId}`. The `StudyGroup` document includes a `memberIds` field that lists the UIDs of all members. 

**Authorization Independence and QAPs:**

*   The structure achieves Authorization Independence by relying on path-based ownership for User Profiles and storing member UIDs within StudyGroup documents.
*   QAPs (Rules are not Filters) are supported via structural segregation for User Profiles and a membership map within StudyGroup documents.

This design facilitates simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules, aligning with the core design principles.
